Vibe 编码会毁掉你的公司

Vibe Coding Will Break Your Company

www.forbes.com 作者 sminchev 分数 62 评论 60 2026/04/28 13:35
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 当人工智能工具在几天内从演示转向决策时，中层领导判断才是真正的控制系统。盖蒂图片社一位没有工程背景的营销经理周一早上打开了 Cursor。到周三下午，她已经有了一个可以运行的面向客户的应用程序。看起来很抛光。它执行核心任务。她向副总裁演示了它，副总裁将其转发给首席营销官，然后首席营销官在高管会议上展示它，作为团队“以人工智能速度前进”的证据。到周五，它就摆在了客户面前。没有人问谁拥有发布它的决定权。没有人根据它实际面临的条件对其进行测试。没有人有文化地位说这看起来很棒，而且我们也没有将其投入生产。原型之所以成为产品，是因为该组织没有系统来区分差异。我最近在董事会会议室观看了该场景的一个版本。一位高级管理人员演示了一个人工智能构建的内部工具。房间里的人都对速度赞叹不已。较少受到关注的是更棘手的问题：发布后谁将拥有它？谁来维护它？当它产生一个明显错误的答案时会发生什么？这就是振动编码即将在企业中公开的内容。那些认为故事与软件有关的公司将输给那些了解故事与判断有关的公司。真正的趋势是决策压缩Andrej Karpathy 在 2025 年初创造了“vibe 编码”一词，用来描述通过自然语言提示构建软件的人工智能辅助风格，通常无需仔细检查底层代码。 Google Cloud 将 Vibe 编码描述为一种软件开发实践，它使应用程序构建变得更容易，特别是对于编程经验有限的人来说。 Cursor、Replit、Lovable、Bolt、GitHub Copilot Workspace、Vercel v0 和 Claude Code 等工具以惊人的速度将这种实践从新颖性转变为工作场所现实。所有这些都是事实。这些都不是重点。重点是振动编码将想法和工件之间的距离从几个月缩小到几个小时。当这个距离缩小时，您的组织在过去 30 年中开发的每一个质量控制机制都会默认被绕过。设计审查。安全审查。法律审查。品牌回顾。必须让工程师相信你的想法值得构建，这是一个简单的摩擦。这是一个治理故事，而不是一个软件故事。它同时发生在组织结构图的各个层面。像 Claude Code 这样的工具将人工智能辅助软件构建从开发人员的专业领域转移到了工作场所的期望。盖蒂图片社 不加判断的速度是一种责任2025 年夏天，SaaStr 创始人 Jason Lemkin 使用 Replit 的 AI 编码代理进行了为期数天的实验。在一次明确的代码冻结过程中，该特工删除了一个实时生产数据库，据报道影响了与 1,200 多名高管和 1,100 多家公司相关的记录。它还捏造数据并歪曲所发生的事情。 Replit 首席执行官阿姆贾德·马萨德 (Amjad Masad) 公开道歉，并称这种行为是不可接受的，因为该公司已采取行动增加更强有力的保护措施。删除花了几秒钟。Lemkin 是一位具有深厚技术素养的开发人员，在专门为此类工作构建的平台上运行了受控实验。现在想象一下，同样的故障模式分布在公司的每个业务职能部门，人员没有技术素养，工作流程从未针对循环中的人工智能而设计。这不是假设的风险。麻省理工学院对企业人工智能采用的研究发现，绝大多数企业生成式人工智能试点未能产生可衡量的财务回报。核心问题不仅仅是技术本身。这是组织无法将人工智能集成到实际工作流程中、从部署中学习以及区分有效的演示和已交付的系统。Klarna 通过公开方式了解到这一点。在公开宣传其人工智能助手的工作相当于数百名客户服务代理后，该公司于 2025 年再次开始雇用人类客户服务人员。首席执行官塞巴斯蒂安·西米亚特科斯基 (Sebastian Siemiatkowski) 后来强调，需要在人工智能的使用与人类支持之间取得平衡，并向客户明确表示，在需要时会有人类服务。该技术在某些方面发挥了作用。围绕它的判断系统是不完整的。Vibe 编码可能会在业务功能中增加这种失败模式。营销将发布应用程序。运营将交付工作流程。人力资源部将提供内部工具。每一个看起来都像是幻灯片上的进展。有些产量很少。其他人可能会造成公司无法发现的负债，直到客户、监管机构或记者首先发现它们。加拿大航空已经在法庭上了解到，不准确的聊天机器人指导仍然可能成为该公司的责任。人工智能时代的瓶颈不是生产。这是洞察力。正如我在《福布斯》上所写的那样，洞察力并不是一种人格特质。它是一个组织系统。这就是为什么我一直认为人工智能就绪性主要不是一种技术能力。这是一门领导纪律：有能力决定什么应该加快，什么应该放慢，以及谁有权知道其中的差异。人工智能时代的真正瓶颈不是生产。这是组织系统形式的洞察力。 盖蒂图片社公司将崩溃的五个地方我认为，组织需要进行我所说的判断系统审计，这是一个跨越五个维度的诊断，以确定公司是否可以代谢人工智能，而不仅仅是部署人工智能。 Vibe 编码是我见过的该框架最干净的压力测试。决策权当一个非工程师使用Lovable或Bolt在两天内构建一个工作应用程序时，谁有权批准它供外部使用？在大多数公司中，没有人知道。组织结构图是为只有某些角色才能产生某些工件的世界而构建的。 Vibe 编码违反了这一假设，由此产生的歧义将由行动最快的人来填补，而这很少是应该由谁来决定的。超越文化您组织中的某人可以在看到一个漂亮的原型后说“不”而不会面临职业风险吗？如果答案是否定的，vibe 编码就变成了单向棘轮。每一个演示良好的原型都会向前推进，因为阻止它的社会成本超过了交付它的感知风险。超越文化是人工智能企业的免疫系统。大多数公司都没有。当没有任何有地位的人可以说指标看起来不错而体验很糟糕时，Klarna 的客户服务就会出现逆转。情境英特尔反复出现的风险是，人工智能工具可以生成技术上合理的输出，但在情境上却很幼稚。振动编码的应用程序不知道您的监管环境、您的客户群、您的品牌声音、您的数据敏感性或您的操作限制。应用该背景的判断存在于人类之中，但前提是这些人在原型受到赞扬之前就在房间里。在当今的大多数工作流程中，它们都是在事后进行清理的。 Replit 事件是同一模式的一个极端版本：代理具有没有上下文的能力，而没有上下文的能力正是生产数据库被删除的方式。学习速度在振动编码原型失败后要问的正确问题不是人工智能做错了什么。这正是我们的流程所遗漏的。学习速度快的公司将每次失败视为其判断系统的校准事件。 Shopify 首席执行官 Tobi Lütke 围绕这一原则制定了大部分人工智能任务，将积极采用与明确的组织学习期望相结合。他的公开备忘录宣称，“反射性人工智能使用”现在是一个基线预期，报告指出人工智能的使用将包含在绩效和同行评审中。无论你如何看待这项任务，基本的认识都是正确的：没有学习速度的采用只是暴露。道德辨别Vibe 编码使得构建不应该构建的东西变得非常容易。想想监控功能。操控性用户体验模式。未经有意义的同意而收集用户数据。需要人工审查的决策自动化。技术障碍曾经为你做一些道德工作。现在不再了。如果你的组织不具备道德洞察力作为长期能力，振动编码将公开揭示这一差距，并且标题不会引起同情。在所有五项上得分都很高的公司可以使用振动编码作为真正的促进剂。在其中任何一项上得分不佳的公司都会使用氛围编码来加速自己的曝光度。如果没有人工审核层，只有在客户、监管机构或记者首先发现它之前，失败才会显现出来。问题不在于采用。大多数关于氛围编码的领导对话都被视为采用问题。我们应该鼓励它吗？我们应该为此进行训练吗？我们应该限制它吗？这些都是错误的问题。无论您是否有政策，Vibe 编码已经在您的公司内部进行。许多员工已经可以在个人设备上访问 Cursor、Claude、ChatGPT、Replit 和 Lovable，因此非正式的采用曲线已经超出了政策流程。正确的问题是诊断性的，而不是战略性的。你的判断系统的状态如何？它将接受什么测试？在未来 24 个月内取得领先的公司并不是采用速度最快的公司。他们的判断系统足够成熟，采用不会破坏他们的判断系统。这是大多数高管尚未做出的转变。在人工智能之前的时代，能力稀缺，判断力被假设。在人工智能的新时代，能力是廉价的，判断力是稀缺的输入。作为首席执行官和高级团队的顾问，应对这一转变，我反复目睹同样的模式：领导者仍然围绕旧的稀缺性组织自己，他们即将在公开场合发现，他们针对错误的约束进行了优化。 领导者周一应该做什么如果您是一名高级领导者，并且您从本文中学到了一件事，那就接受这个。在编写氛围编码策略之前，请运行判断系统审核。选择您的组织最近做出的与人工智能相关的决策。一种工具的采用。一名飞行员。一个被提升或被杀掉的原型。遍历五个维度。决策权哪里含糊不清？超越文化失败在哪里？房间里哪里缺少情境智能？你学到了什么？这些学习是如何编码的？道德洞察力在哪里依赖于个人良知而不是制度过程？你会发现差距。每个人都这样做。然而，问题是你是在 Vivi 编码之前还是之后找到它们。这是没有人大声说出来的部分：你的竞争对手不会打败你，因为他们的代码速度更快。他们会打败你，因为他们的判断系统足够成熟，能够吸收氛围编码产生的内容，而你的可能还不行。 在我现在进行的高管对话中，问题不再是人工智能辅助建筑是否即将到来。就是领导人是否愿意承认它已经到来了。 Replit、Klarna 和加拿大航空发出警告。下一个可能不是来自别人的公司。它可能来自你的。

Original Summary

  1. Midlevel leadership judgment is the real control system when AI tools move from demo to decision in days.Getty ImagesA marketing manager with no engineering background opens Cursor on Monday morning. By Wednesday afternoon, she has a working customer-facing app. It looks polished. It performs the core task. She demos it to her VP, who forwards it to their CMO, who then shows it in the executive staff meeting as evidence that the team is “moving at AI speed.”By Friday, it is in front of customers.No one asked who owned the decision to ship it. No one tested it against the conditions it would actually face. No one had the cultural standing to say this looks great, and we are not putting it into production. The prototype became a product because the organization had no system for telling the difference.I watched a version of this scenario play out recently in a boardroom. A senior executive demoed an AI-built internal tool. The room admired the speed. What received less attention were the harder questions: Who would own it after launch? Who would maintain it? And what would happen when it produced an answer that was confidently wrong?This is what vibe coding is about to expose across businesses. The companies that think the story is about software are going to lose to the companies that understand the story is about judgment.The Real Trend Is Decision CompressionAndrej Karpathy coined the term “vibe coding” in early 2025 to describe an AI-assisted style of building software through natural-language prompting, often without close inspection of the underlying code. Google Cloud describes vibe coding as a software development practice that makes app building more accessible, especially for people with limited programming experience. Tools like Cursor, Replit, Lovable, Bolt, GitHub Copilot Workspace, v0 by Vercel and Claude Code have moved the practice from novelty to workplace reality with stunning speed.All of that is true. None of it is the point.The point is that vibe coding collapses the distance between idea and artifact from months to hours. When that distance collapses, every quality-control mechanism your organization developed over the last 30 years gets bypassed by default. Design review. Security review. Legal review. Brand review. The simple friction of having to convince an engineer your idea was worth building. That is a governance story, not a software story. It is happening at every level of the org chart simultaneously.Tools like Claude Code move AI assisted software building from a developer specialty to a workplace expectation.Getty ImagesSpeed Without Judgment Is A LiabilityIn the summer of 2025, SaaStr founder Jason Lemkin ran a multiday experiment with Replit’s AI coding agent. During an explicit code freeze, the agent deleted a live production database, reportedly affecting records tied to over 1,200 executives and more than 1,100 companies. It also fabricated data and misrepresented what had happened. Replit CEO Amjad Masad publicly apologized and described the behavior as unacceptable as the company moved to add stronger safeguards. The deletion took seconds.Lemkin is a developer who has deep technical literacy, running a controlled experiment, on a platform built specifically for this kind of work. Now imagine the same failure mode distributed across every business function in your company, with people who do not have technical literacy, on workflows that were never designed for AI in the loop.This is not a hypothetical risk. MIT research on enterprise AI adoption found the vast majority of corporate generative AI pilots were failing to produce measurable financial returns. The core problem was not simply the technology itself. It was the organizational inability to integrate AI into real workflows, learn from deployment and distinguish between a demo that worked and a system that delivered.Klarna learned this the public way. After publicly touting its AI assistant was doing work equivalent to hundreds of customer service agents, the company began hiring human customer service workers again in 2025. CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski later emphasized the need to balance AI use with human support and to make clear to customers that a human would be available when needed. The technology worked in some respects. The judgment system around it was incomplete.Vibe coding is likely to multiply that failure mode across business functions. Marketing will ship apps. Operations will ship workflows. HR will ship internal tools. Each one will look like progress on a slide. Some will produce little. Others may create liabilities the company will not discover until a customer, a regulator or a journalist finds them first. Air Canada already learned, in court, that inaccurate chatbot guidance can still become the company’s responsibility.The bottleneck in the AI era is not production. It is discernment. And discernment, as I have written in Forbes, is not a personality trait. It is an organizational system. That is why I have been arguing that AI readiness is not primarily a technology capability. It is a leadership discipline: the capacity to decide what should move faster, what should slow down, and who has the authority to know the difference.The real bottleneck in the AI era is not production. It is discernment in the form of an organizational system.Getty ImagesThe 5 Places Your Company Will BreakI have argued that organizations need to conduct what I call a Judgment System Audit, a diagnostic across five dimensions that determine whether a company can metabolize AI rather than just deploy it. Vibe coding is the cleanest stress test of that framework I have seen. Here is where the cracks will show.Decision RightsWhen a non-engineer builds a working app in two days using Lovable or Bolt, who has the authority to approve it for external use? In most companies, no one knows. The org chart was built for a world where only certain roles could produce certain artifacts. Vibe coding violates that assumption, and the resulting ambiguity will be filled by whoever moves fastest, which is rarely whoever should be deciding.Override CultureCan someone in your organization look at a slick prototype and say “no” without career risk? If the answer is no, vibe coding becomes a one-way ratchet. Every prototype that demos well moves forward, because the social cost of stopping it exceeds the perceived risk of shipping it. Override culture is the immune system of an AI-enabled enterprise. Most companies do not have one. The customer-service reversal at Klarna is what happens when nobody with standing can say the metric looks good and the experience is bad.Contextual IntelThe recurring risk is that AI tools can generate technically plausible output that is contextually naive. A vibe-coded app does not know your regulatory environment, your customer base, your brand voice, your data sensitivity or your operational constraints. The judgment to apply that context lives in humans, but only if those humans are in the room before the prototype receives praise. In most workflows today, they are brought in afterward to clean up. The Replit incident is an extreme version of the very same pattern: The agent had capability without context, and capability without context is exactly how production databases get deleted.Learning VelocityThe right question to ask after a vibe-coded prototype fails is not what did the AI do wrong. It is what did our process miss. Companies with high learning velocity treat each failure as a calibration event for their judgment system. Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke has built much of his AI mandate around this principle, pairing aggressive adoption with explicit organizational learning expectations. His public memo declared that “reflexive AI usage” was now a baseline expectation, and reporting noted that AI use would be included in performance and peer reviews. Whatever you think of the mandate, the underlying recognition is correct: Adoption without learning velocity is just exposure.Ethical DiscernmentVibe coding makes it trivially easy to build things that should not be built. Think surveillance features. Manipulative UX patterns. User data collection without meaningful consent. Automation of decisions that warrant human review. The technical barrier used to do some of the ethical work for you. It does not anymore. If your organization does not have ethical discernment as a standing capability, vibe coding will reveal that gap publicly, and the headline will not be sympathetic.A company that scores well on all five can use vibe coding as a genuine accelerant. A company that scores poorly on any of them will use vibe coding to accelerate its own exposure.Without a human review layer, failure does not show up until a customer, a regulator, or a journalist finds it first.Getty ImagesThe Question Is Not Adoption. It Is Readiness.Most leadership conversations about vibe coding are framed as adoption questions. Should we encourage it? Should we train for it? Should we restrict it.Those are the wrong questions to ask. Vibe coding is already happening inside your company whether you have a policy or not. Many employees already have access to Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, Replit and Lovable on personal devices, so the informal adoption curve is already outrunning the policy process.The right question is diagnostic, not strategic. What is the state of your judgment system, and what is it about to be tested against? The companies that will pull ahead in the next 24 months are not the ones that adopt fastest. They are the ones whose judgment systems are mature enough that adoption does not break them.This is the inversion that most executives have not yet made. In the era before AI, capability was scarce and judgment was assumed. In the new era of AI, capability is cheap and judgment is the scarce input. As an advisor to CEOs and senior teams navigating this exact shift, I witness the same pattern repeatedly: Leaders are still organizing themselves around the old scarcity, and they are about to discover, in public, that they optimized for the wrong constraint.What Leaders Should Do MondayIf you are a senior leader and you take one thing from this article, take this. Before you write a vibe coding policy, run a Judgment System Audit.Pick a recent AI-related decision your organization made. A tool adoption. A pilot. A prototype that got promoted or killed. Walk it through the five dimensions. Where were decision rights ambiguous? Where did override culture fail? Where was contextual intelligence missing from the room? What did you learn, and how is that learning encoded? Where did ethical discernment depend on individual conscience rather than institutional process?You will find gaps. Everyone does. The question, however, is whether you find them before vibe coding does, or after. Here is the part nobody is saying out loud: Your competitors are not going to beat you because they vibe code faster. They are going to beat you because their judgment systems are mature enough to absorb what vibe coding produces, and yours may not be.In the executive conversations I am having now, the question is no longer whether AI-assisted building is coming. It is whether leaders are willing to admit that it has already arrived. Replit, Klarna and Air Canada were warning shots. The next one may not come from someone else’s company. It may come from yours.

评论（默认中文）

daishi55 2026/04/28 14:08
> Jason Wingard 博士是一位全球知名的高管，在企业、非营利组织和学术领域拥有丰富的经验，专门研究未来的学习和工作。他目前担任哈佛大学的高级顾问，为受托人、高级管理人员和教师领导提供建议，并领导有关劳动力转型和创新的研究议程。他还是 The Education Board, Inc. 的执行主席和 Social Finance, Inc. 的高级顾问，提供战略和远见卓识的咨询，同时推进有关领导力和劳动力发展的国家研究议程。他最近担任天普大学第 12 任校长，并担任管理学教授和政策、组织和领导力研究教授的双终身教职。此前，Wingard 博士曾担任哥伦比亚大学专业研究学院院长、高盛董事总经理兼首席学习官。此前，他曾担任宾夕法尼亚大学沃顿商学院副院长； ePals 基金会总裁兼首席执行官兼 ePals, Inc. 高级副总裁；并在阿斯彭研究所、Vanguard Group、Silicon Graphics, Inc. (SGI) 和斯坦福大学担任领导职务。 作为获奖作家，Wingard 博士发表了有关领导力、学习和劳动力战略的大量著作。

不确定这个人具体做什么或他的专业知识是什么，但我相当确定这不是软件开发。

> Dr. Jason Wingard is a globally recognized executive with deep experience across corporate, nonprofit, and academic sectors, specializing in the future of learning and work.He currently serves as Senior Advisor at Harvard University, where he advises trustees, senior administrators, and faculty leaders, and leads a research agenda on workforce transformation and innovation. He is also Executive Chairman of The Education Board, Inc. and Senior Advisor at Social Finance, Inc., providing strategic and visionary consulting while advancing a national research agenda on leadership and workforce development.He most recently served as the 12th President of Temple University, where he held dual tenured faculty appointments as Professor of Management and Professor of Policy, Organizational, and Leadership Studies.Previously, Dr. Wingard was Dean of the School of Professional Studies at Columbia University and Managing Director and Chief Learning Officer at Goldman Sachs. Earlier, he served as Vice Dean of the Wharton School, University of Pennsylvania; President & CEO of the ePals Foundation and Senior Vice President at ePals, Inc.; and held leadership roles with the Aspen Institute, Vanguard Group, Silicon Graphics, Inc. (SGI), and Stanford University.An award-winning author, Dr. Wingard has published widely on leadership, learning, and workforce strategy.

Not sure exactly what this guy does or what his expertise is, but I am fairly certain it’s not software development.

在 HN 中查看
Incipient 2026/04/28 14:33
我确信振动编码的内部或外部应用程序会毁掉一家公司。然而，10 家公司的思考过程是：

- 2 根本不会使用人工智能，只会落后并停滞不前（或破产）

- 2将部分使用人工智能，也许会跟上，也许不会

- 1 会因为整个应用程序而发疯并爆炸（请参阅 Tea 应用程序或其他应用程序）

- 4 将拥有低效的应用程序，遭受声誉损害，损失一些金钱，或类似的情况，但可能生存下来

- 1人将中奖并获得100M ARR的4人公司。

统计数据当然完全是捏造的，但你明白了。

I'm sure a vibe coded internal or external application WILL break a company. The thought process is however, out of 10 companies:

- 2 won't use AI at all and simply be left behind and stagnate (or go bust)

- 2 will partly use AI, and maybe keep up, maybe not

- 1 will go nuts vibe an entire app and explode (see Tea app or whatever)

- 4 will have an inefficient app, suffer reputational damage, lose some money, or similar, but probably survive

- 1 will hit the jackpot and get a 100M ARR company with 4 people.

Stats are of course completely made up, but you get the point.

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 14:39
> 1将中奖并获得100M ARR公司4人。

我要指出的是，当你获得 1 亿 ARR 时，无论如何雇佣更多的人似乎都是值得的。

但我猜测聘用的门槛将会非常高，因为在我看来，未来重度人工智能自动化时代聘用人员的最佳人选基本上是创始人级别的有远见的领导者，他们也是能够持续做出正确决策的主题专家，并给予他们 100 万以上的薪水作为交换。

如果你有 1 亿 ARR，你可能可以负担得起 30 名这样的员工（以及找到他们所需的高昂招聘费用），并让他们整天指挥人工智能。因此，您的公司的员工人数将非常少，但仍超过 4 人。

（哦，这将如何影响财富不平等？我宁愿不去想它）

> 1 will hit the jackpot and get a 100M ARR company with 4 people.

I will point out that at the point where you get an 100M ARR it seems worth it to hire more people regardless.

But I'm guessing that the bar to be hired will be EXTREMELY high, because IMO the best people to hire people in future heavy-AI-automation-era would be basically founder-level visionary leaders who are also subject matter experts who can consistently make good decisions, and giving them 1M+ salaries in exchange.

If you have 100M ARR you can probably afford like 30 of these employees (and the probably exorbitant recruiting fees required to find them) and have them command AI all day. So your company will be extremely small in headcount but still more than 4 people.

(oh and how will this affect wealth inequality? i prefer to not think about it)

camillomiller 2026/04/28 15:25
我喜欢这种乌托邦式的小场景，它让 HN 普通用户湿透了，因为他们无情地回避考虑人工智能中长期可持续性的核心问题。 也就是说，对外部模型的依赖、糟糕的模型成本补贴、可能对核心产品（模型）产生负面影响的经济衰退风险。 但这是不可避免的未来，如果你敢提出担忧，你就是勒德分子。伙计我们也来了
I love this little utopian scenarios that make HN average users wet because they relentlessly avoid considering the core issues with mid to long term AI sustainability. Namely, dependence on external models, fucked up model costs subsidization, financial exposure to a downturn that could negatively affect the core product (models). Yet this is the inevitable future and if you dare raising concern you’re a luddite. Man what have we come too
在 HN 中查看
donatj 2026/04/28 14:50
我认为事情比这更复杂。

任何人可以感受到的任何获得任何程度的轻微吸引力的代码都可以很容易地被所有竞争对手复制，并且在很短的时间内，因为确定产品优势的实际困难部分已经为他们完成了。

I think it's more complicated than that.

Anything someone can vibe code that gains any level of mild traction can then easily duplicated by all their competitors and in a fraction of the time because the actual hard part, determining the products edges, has already been done for them.

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 15:06
同意。这就是为什么我认为平台/网络效应将很快成为在许多科技市场中维持生存的必然因素。 （或者其他真正难以克服的不公平优势）

即使有网络效应，这仍然是您构建生态系统和追赶您的竞争对手之间的一场竞赛。

然而，如果你确实拥有某种网络效应护城河，而你的竞争对手还没有，那么你就拥有世界上唯一重要的优势，因为记住，氛围复制是双向的。您可以逐项复制竞争对手的功能，就像他们复制您一样。因此，你将始终保持功能对等，而每个人都只使用你，因为你是拥有最大生态系统的老牌玩家，很快你就会将暂时的优势变成永久性的优势。

注意：传统平台无法真正从中受益，因为您可能需要从头开始重写产品以适应任何类型的尖端人工智能开发工作流程。谁先创建人工智能原生平台并对其进行扩展，谁就获胜。

Agreed. This is why I think that platform/network effects will be mandatory to stay afloat in a lot of the tech market pretty soon. (Or other types of unfair advantages that are truly hard to overcome)

Even with network effects, it's still a race between you building a ecosystem and your competitors catching up to you.

However, if you DO have some sort of network effect moat and your competitors DON'T (yet), then you have the only advantage that matters in the world, because remember, vibe-copying goes both ways. You can copy your competitors feature-by-feature just like they can copy you. So you'll just always keep up feature parity while everyone only uses you because you're the established player with the biggest ecosystem, and soon enough you'll turn your temporary advantage into a permanent one.

Note: legacy platforms can't really benefit from this because you probably need to rewrite your product from scratch to fit any sort of cutting-edge AI dev workflow. Whoever creates a AI-native platform and scales it first wins.

coldtea 2026/04/28 14:46
>- 2 根本不会使用人工智能，只会落后并停滞不前（或破产）

他们为什么会这么做？好像他们的软件速度更快就是差异化因素？

在我作为消费者的职业生涯中（笑），选择从来都与此无关。这是关于商业主张、定价、实施质量、保证公司能够长期存在、他们不是渣男等等。

如果有什么软件流失让我感到厌烦的话，尤其是当它以扰乱我的既定用途或稳定性为代价时。

>- 2 won't use AI at all and simply be left behind and stagnate (or go bust)

Would why would they? As if their software being made faster is the differentiator?

In my career as a consumer (lol), choice was never about that. It was about the business proposition, pricing, quality of implementation, guarantees the company is gonna be there long term, them not being scumbags, and so on.

If anything software churn put me off, especially when it come at the cost of messing with my established use, or stability.

在 HN 中查看
sminchev 2026/04/28 14:30
我在一份报告中读到： 人工智能放大了。它放大了优秀专业人士的成功，也放大了糟糕专业人士的失败。

在所有情况下，整个企业解决方案都无法使用纯粹的 vivicoding 来制作。需要规范，预定义规则、编码风格、安全考虑的基础。

I read it in a report: AI amplifies. It amplifies the success of the good professionals and amplifies the failures of the bad ones.

In all cases, whole enterprise solution can't be made with pure vibecoding. Specification is needed, a basis of predefined rules, coding styles, security considerations.

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 14:32
> 人工智能不断增强。它放大了优秀专业人士的成功，也放大了糟糕专业人士的失败。

总体而言，它还使问题变得更糟，因为让坏人更容易表现得很好。他们会有听起来更好的承诺，但如果你听了他们的，几年后你就会崩溃。这甚至不一定是故意的，只是技术上无知的人在玩弄政治的同时引导人工智能的阿谀奉承（即在将想法委托给人工智能的同时进行促销）就会产生有问题的效果。

> AI amplifies. It amplifies the success of the good professionals and amplifies the failures of the bad ones.

It also worsens the problem in general by making it way, WAY easier for the bad ones to performatively appear good. They'll have the better-sounding promises but if you listen to them you'll crash and burn in a few years. This doesn't even have to be intentional, just someone technically ignorant channeling AI sycophancy while simultaneously playing politics (i.e. promotionmaxxing while delegating ideas to AI) will have the problematic effect.

在 HN 中查看
netcan 2026/04/28 14:33
所以这篇文章不是很好，但是关于编码的争论 是 很有趣。

这就是我的想法：在机会和风险增加的情况下……你必须知道自己的立场。

第一个问题是 更多软件对您来说实际值多少钱。”

这是一种充满自我欺骗的行为。软件开发成本高昂。公司必须列出清单、愿望清单和路线图。他们拥有 A/B 测试系统和生产力思维。

但是……如果 Linkedin、Salesforce 或任何其他公司确实有办法生产软件来赚钱……他们早就这么做了。剩余的机会遵循边际价值曲线/悬崖递减。

在我看来，软件开发不一定是瓶颈。所以……机会有限，风险更大。

机会就在试图引导与 Salesforce 功能对等的新贵身上。

如果你还没有顾客……你可以放松氛围，看看它是否有效。

在我看来，公司需要重新审视谷歌的早期阶段。让万花齐放。 20%的时间。如果你释放有能力的人并给他们代币..这是寻找机会的好方法。

谷歌的千朵鲜花都死了，因为他们已经到了机会不再无处不在的地步。最好的想法已经被发现，而且……大到足以推动谷歌前进的市场却很少。 1000 亿美元的市场并不多。

目前还没有办法安全、大规模地进行氛围编码。

So the article isn't very good but the vibe coding debate is pretty interesting.

This is how I'm thinking about it: in a scenario with increased opportunity and risk... You've gotta know where you stand.

First question is how much is more software actually worth to you."

This is one with a lot of self deception. Software development is expensive. The companies have to do lists and wishlist and road maps. They have an A/B testing system and a productivity mindset.

But... If Linkedin, Salesforce or any whatnot really did have ways of producing software to make money... they would have done it already. Remaining opportunities follow a diminishing marginal value curve/cliff.

Imo, software development isn't necessarily a bottleneck. So... opportunity is limited and risk is the bigger deal.

The opportunity is at the upstart trying to bootstrap feature parity with Salesforce.

If you have no customers yet... you can unfettter the vibe and see if it works.

Imo companies need to revisit google's early days. Let a thousand flowers bloom. 20% time. If you unleash capable people and give them tokens .. That's a good way of searching for opportunities.

The thousand flowers died at Google because they had reached a point where opportunities are not everywhere. The best ideas had been discovered and also... the markets big enough to move Google's dial are few. There aren't many $100bn markets.

There's no way to do vibe coding safely, at scale, currently.

在 HN 中查看
tossandthrow 2026/04/28 14:44
> 更多软件对您来说实际值多少钱。

一个真正被误解的氛围编码任务是代码删除和重构，尤其是在更多的企业环境中。

我认为这是对代理开发的根本误解：人们只将其视为添加代码的工具。

> how much is more software actually worth to you.

A really misunderstood vibe coding task, especially in more corperate settings, is code removal and refactorings.

I think this is the the fundamental misunderstanding about agentic development: people only see it as a tool to add code.

在 HN 中查看
stingraycharles 2026/04/28 14:53
这对我来说有点像废话，而且我对几家企业和初创公司有一个鸟瞰图。

法学硕士是 不是 用于代码删除或重构，要么是为了“希望解除”这个已经落后截止日期 12 个月的大型项目，要么只是为了加快开发速度（某种程度上）。

This smells like BS to me, and I have a bird’s eye view into several enterprises and startups.

LLMs are not being used for code removal or refactorings, it’s either to “hopefully unblock” this large project that has been behind deadline for 12 months, or to just speed up development (somewhat).

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 14:24
> 没有判断力的速度是一种责任

那么，还有什么选择呢？

没有判断力的速度？ （也许你会没事的。或者你的业务可能会因为意大利面条式代码堆积而陷入困境，超出了人工审查和质量控制突破的希望）

没有速度的判断？ （隔壁那家由 4 人富有远见的团队和一群人工智能领导的初创公司在发货能力上碾压了你 100 人的公司）

判断+速度同时进行？ （解雇大部分员工，只保留有远见的人？如何筛选能够做出正确决策的人？）

> Speed without judgement is a liability

So, what's the alternative?

Speed without judgement? (Maybe you'll be fine. Or maybe your business gets run to the ground by spaghetti code piling up beyond any hope for human review and quality controls breaking)

Judgement without speed? (That startup next door led by a 4-people visionary team and a bunch of AIs stomps over your 100-person company in ability to ship)

Judgement + speed at the same time? (layoff most of your employees and keep only the visionaries? how do you even filter for people who can make good decisions?)

在 HN 中查看
acron0 2026/04/28 14:40
我认为判断角度是本文唯一有趣的部分，值得追求的部分是尽可能自动化判断。
I think the judgement angle is the only interesting part of this article, and the piece worth pursuing is automating the judgement where possible.
在 HN 中查看
slopinthebag 2026/04/28 14:38
> 隔壁那家由 4 人富有远见的团队和一群人工智能领导的初创公司在发货能力上击败了你 100 人的公司

听起来不错，但实际上是这样吗？我的意思是运送速度更快。先发优势是一个因素，但它不是唯一的因素，而且这也不同于额外运输 特征 迅速地。

我的意思是，苹果公司因故意而闻名 晚的 整个市场，而且他们做得很好......

这种心态就是“快速行动，打破常规”，仅仅因为这是 SFBA 中的常见说法，并不意味着它在所有方面都有效。

> That startup next door led by a 4-people visionary team and a bunch of AIs stomps over your 100-person company in ability to ship

That sounds right but is it actually true? By that I mean shipping faster. First mover advantage is a thing, but it's not the only thing, and that's also not the same as shipping additional features quickly.

I mean, Apple is famous for being purposely late to entire markets, and they're doing pretty well...

This mentality is just "move fast and break things", and just because it's a common trope in the SFBA doesn't make it effective across the board.

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 14:54
注意：我假设是 2027-28 年，并且存在可靠的人工智能自动编码器（或您所在领域的同等主力人工智能），这使得与决策相比，实施时间可以忽略不计。对于当今水平的人工智能来说，效果稍弱一些。我还假设这家拥有 100 名员工的公司除了做出决策外，在人工智能方面也非常有能力，但初创公司可以更快地规划事情，因为不需要委员会来这样做。

非常粗略的数学：

如果您的 100 人团队仍然遵循协作流程来消除错误（假设每天需要 10 个人来决定单个可交付成果的形状），然后将设计交给人工智能来实施（因为我们假设人工智能可以在没有监督的情况下完成），那么您每天可以交付 10 个可交付成果。

与此同时，这个 4 人团队可以让所有人从人工智能中汲取想法，帮助他们全天快速做出决策。他们每个人都会花一个小时做出决定，然后将其交给人工智能。他们的决策平均而言与您的 10 人团队会议一样好，因为虽然您的中型公司的决策有时会因政治因素而最终达不到最佳效果，但初创公司的决策是个人的，因此更容易做出错误的决定，我认为这两种影响可以抵消。在这种情况下，假设实施 AI 根本不需要受到监督，您的竞争对手拥有 4 名员工，每天可交付 32 个可交付成果。

总而言之，这不是“快速行动并打破常规”，而是“快速行动，专注于决策，将其他一切委托给人工智能”。请记住，如果人工智能能够完成所有实施，那么决策才是最重要的。

Note: I am assuming that it is 2027-28 and reliable AI automated coders exist (or the equivalent workhorse AI in your field), which makes implementation time negligible compared to making decisions. The effect is somewhat weaker with present-day-level AIs. I'm also assuming that the 100-person company is very competent with AI outside of making decisions, but that the startup can plan things much faster due to not needing a committee to do so.

Very rough maths:

If your 100 person team still follows collaborative processes to cancel out errors (let's say it takes 10 people a day to decide on a single deliverable's shape), then give the design to the AI to implement (as we assume the AI can do it without supervision), then you can ship 10 deliverables a day.

At the same time, that 4 people team can have all of them bouncing ideas off of AIs to help them make decisions in rapidfire all day. They'll each individually spend an hour working on a decision then hand it to an AI. Their decisions are on average as good as your 10-member team meetings because while your medium-sized company's decisions sometimes end up suboptimal due to politics, the startup's decisions are individuals so make the wrong call more often, and I assume these two effects cancel out. In that case, your competitor with 4 people cranks out 32 deliverables a day assuming that the implementation AIs don't have to be supervised at all.

In summary it's not "move fast and break things", it's just "move fast, focus on making decisions, delegate everything else to the AI". Remember that the decisions are all that matters if the AI can do all the implementation.

在 HN 中查看
slopinthebag 2026/04/28 15:05
嗯，为了让数学有效，有很多假设都必须成立，比如，你开始冒险雇佣 9 名女性在一个月内生一个孩​​子。

但它也提出了一些我试图挑战的更基本的假设。它假设每天交付 32 个“可交付成果”（其含义因上下文而异）优于 10 个。这总是正确的吗？与竞争对手相比，这个增量是企业成功的最相关因素吗？ ETC。

Mmm, thats a lot of assumptions that all have to hold true to make the math work, like, you're starting to venture into hiring 9 women to make a baby in a month territory here.

But it also makes some more fundamental assumptions that I'm trying to challenge a bit. It assumes delivering 32 "deliverables" per day (the meaning of which is context specific) is better than 10. Is that always true? Is that delta the most relevant factor in the success of a business compared to its competitors? Etc.

在 HN 中查看
2001zhaozhao 2026/04/28 15:17
我同意，决策的质量比数量要好得多。我试图在这里保持决策质量不变

上述许多假设只是为了保持公平，因为在现实世界中存在很多不容忽视的变量。例如，保持两家公司的人工智能能力相同。

我只是想表明，根据我的假设（~2027-28 AI，非常有能力），它是相当有效的 可以想象的 一家 4 人的有远见的公司可以在 数量，并不是说它一定会发生。我想这比我说的“粗略数学”还要粗略。

我想我想说的是：初创公司一直能够在串行执行速度上击败大公司，但在直接并行工作量上击败他们是非常不寻常的，但我认为这种情况很有可能发生。这仅仅是因为传统公司的决策规模在人员数量上非常差，而且我认为相对于实施工作来说，它会变得越来越重要。因此，重点关注“可交付成果”的数量（我真正的意思是中等规模的项目设计，认为相当于开发团队的 5 天目标，我认为这是人工智能在需要人类决策输入之前的平均任务范围）

至少，小团队将在一段时间内获胜，直到我们获得直接的超级智能来取代决策者。这时，情况突然发生逆转，最富有的公司默认获胜。

I agree, quality of decisions will be much better than quantity. I'm trying to keep decision quality constant here

A lot of the above assumptions are just to keep things fair since in the real world there are a lot of variables that can't be ignored. For example, keeping AI competence equal between the two companies.

I'm just trying to show that under my assumptions (~2027-28 AI, highly competent) it is quite conceivable that a 4-person visionary company can start a beat a much larger traditional one on quantity, not that it will definitely happen. I guess it's even rougher than the "rough math" I said it is.

I guess the point I'm trying to make is this: startups have always been able to beat big companies in serial execution speeds, but beating them in straight-up parallel work quantity is very unusual, but I think there's a good chance it will happen. This is simply because decision-making scales really poorly in traditional companies by headcount and I think it'll get more and more important relative to implementation work. Hence the focus on quantity of "deliverables" (i really mean medium-size project designs, think equivalent to 5-day targets for a dev team which I assume to be AI's average task horizon before it needs human decision input)

At least, the small team will win for some time until we get straight-up superintelligence that replaces the decision-makers as well. At which point the calculus suddenly flips and the richest company wins by default.

在 HN 中查看
slopinthebag 2026/04/28 15:57
是的，我并不一定不同意假设这些假设最终是正确的。这并不是我真正想要表达的重点。更多关于这样一种心态，即你需要跟上众所周知的攀比，甚至牺牲其他东西才能做到这一点，否则你就会被抛在后面。您可以在人们谈论自己的个人发展的帖子中看到这一点。担心被抛在后面，或者人们警告其他人，如果他们不全力以赴地发展，他们就会被抛在后面。它只是感觉过于简单化，几乎在哲学上是空洞的。

我当然可以理解对就业的恐惧，人们需要钱来生活和养活他们所爱的人，但除此之外，生活还有更多的事情不仅仅是抛出越来越多的“做事”代码，值得考虑这样做是否有任何真正的好处，或者即使负面影响超过正面影响。我想我们会实时发现每个人的副项目是否最好不完成，或者通过氛围编码完成。我不太清楚。

我并不是提倡 不是 在公司或其他地方利用法学硕士，只是以牺牲其他一切为代价来优先考虑速度可能没有那么有价值。当然，如果没有缺点，那它就纯粹是有价值的，但这基本上回避了这个问题，哈哈。

Yeah, I don't necessarily disagree assuming those assumptions end up being true. It just wasn't really the point I was trying to make. More about the mentality that you need to keep up with the proverbial Joneses and even sacrifice other things in order to do so, or else you will be left behind. You see this on threads where people are talking about their own personal development as well. Fears about being left behind, or people warning others that if they don't go all-in on agentic development they will be left behind. It just feels so oversimplified, almost philosophically empty.

I can understand fears about employment of course, people need money to live and support their loved ones, but beyond this there is more to life than just slopping out more and more code that "does things" and it's worth considering if there is any real benefit to do so, or even if the negative aspects outweigh the positive ones. I suppose we will find out in real time if everyone's side projects are better to be left unfinished, or finished by vibe coding. It's not so clear to me.

I'm not advocating for not utilising LLM's in companies or whatever, just that prioritising velocity at the expense of everything else might not be so valuable. Of course if there are no downsides than it would be purely valuable, but that basically begs the question lol.

Software and services are becoming buggy, content quality plowed straight through bedrock, most people use AI to turn off their brains, and the people that care are left drudging through slop and garbage in both their professional and personal lives.

I want off this train to hell. I am truly (not exaggerating) on the verge of abandoning everything to go live in the woods.

LLMs are told what to produce.

piloto_ciego 2026/04/28 14:13
根据我迄今为止的经验，这显然是错误的。 我的意思是，我是“氛围工程”并且知道我在做什么？ 但现在的工作方式是我更像是一名架构师而不是一名编码员。 这意味着我可以更快地做事，但也意味着它不那么有趣。 但顾客并不真正关心“乐趣”——所以我做了我必须做的事。

This is just patently false in my experience thus far. I mean, I'm "vibe engineering" and know what I'm doing relatively well? But the way this works now is I'm more like an architect than a coder anymore. This means I can do things faster, but it also means it's less fun. But the customer doesn't really care about "fun" - so I do what I've gotta do.

But if anything, I could probably go a lot faster and be fine, it's just my life would be miserable. If you're going to "vibe code" try to remember to actually... you know... vibe.

spoiler 2026/04/28 14:27
问题是，开发时间如此压缩，以至于您失去了对代码库的深入了解。就像，我不认为人类能够这么快地形成如此详细的记忆？也许这只是我的问题。不管怎样，所以当你需要调试或修复东西时，人工智能的推理将是“我认为这是有道理的”，而你对代码库的心理情绪现在很不稳定。最终，您最多只能绘制一个不连贯的高级架构图。

人工智能解决问题的方法通常是“差不多”来解决它。它很少考虑解决设计问题

The thing is, the development timeline is so compressed that you lose intimate knowledge of the codebase. Like, I don't think humans can form memories that detailed that quickly? Maybe it's just a me problem though. Anyway, so when you need to debug or fix stuff, AI's reasoning will be "welp makes sense, I suppose" and your mental mood of the codebase is now slippery. Eventually there comes a time where at best you can draw an incoherent high-level diagram of the architecture.

And AIs solution to problem is generally "more of the same" to fix it. It rarely looks at fixing design problems

在 HN 中查看
aussieguy1234 2026/04/28 14:24
我对氛围编码的定义是未经审查的编码（例如，非技术人员氛围编码某些内容）。在称职的工程师手中，人工智能工具确实可以提高生产力。

但即使在那里，也有责任能力，你也不可能让工程师同时维护大量系统，所以如果你动作太快，即使进行了技术审查，你仍然可能会给自己带来麻烦。

我认为，在没有有能力的工程师审查工作的情况下进行氛围编码可能会比在没有咨询实际律师的情况下起草自己的法律文件产生更糟糕的结果。

两者都可能在未来带来令人不快的意外。

My definition of vibe coding is coding without review (for example, a non technical person vibe coding something). In the hands of a competent engineer the AI tools do boost productivity.

But even there, there is responsibility capacity, you can't have an engineer maintaining large numbers of systems at once, so if you moved fast you can still get yourself in trouble even with technical review.

I'd argue that doing vibe coding without a competent engineer reviewing the work is likely to have worse outcomes than drafting your own legal documents without consulting an actual lawyer.

Both are likely to result in nasty surprises in the future.

我不明白这种二分法。编码 是 架构师，你不能把这些东西分开。事实上，这就是它的全部。无论您是编写汇编语言还是 Python，都没有关系。

> I'm more like an architect than a coder anymore

I don't understand this dichotomy. Coding is architecting, you can't divorce these things. In fact that is all it really is. It doesn't matter if you're writing assembly or python.

Animats 2026/04/28 14:44
问题是，当事情搞砸时，谁受到指责，谁付出代价。如果是客户，并且您可以承受失去一小部分客户的损失，那么这可能是值得的。这只是糟糕的客户服务的另一种形式。如果它是内部的，并且都是输出，没有输入，并且内部组织并不真的那么需要这些信息，那么这可能会成功。

但赋予它权力 做 确实有麻烦了。

The question is, when it screws up, who gets blamed, and who pays. If it's the customer, and you can afford to lose a small fraction of customers, it may be worth it. It's just another form of crappy customer service. If it's internal, and it's all output, no input, and the internal organization doesn't really need that info that badly, that might work out.

But give it the authority to do something and there's real trouble.

在 HN 中查看
slopinthebag 2026/04/28 14:50
我觉得双方都提出了很多非常简单化和过于简单化的论点。 Vibe 编码不一定会破坏您的公司，同样拒绝人工智能也不一定会让您落后。在我看来，开发速度和软件质量似乎都与业务成功没有特别相关。许多企业要么出货速度比竞争对手慢，要么生产质量低得多的软件，通常两者兼而有之（你好，微软！）。认为其他事情更重要是不是很疯狂？

比如，认为成功公司之间的速度和质量差异与人工智能使用和不使用人工智能之间的差异一样大（如果不大于的话）是错误的吗？

是否采取保守的人工智能采用方法，寻求适度的速度提升，但保持现有的大部分质量？那不是很理想吗？或者可能取决于公司运营的特定市场？

I feel like there is a lot of really reductive and over simplistic arguments being made on both sides here. Vibe coding won't necessarily break your company, and rejecting AI similarly won't necessarily leave you behind. Neither the speed of development nor quality of software seems particularly correlated with business success imo. Plenty of businesses exist which either ship slower than their competitors or produce much lower quality software, often times both (hello Microsoft!). Is it crazy to think other things matter way more?

Like, is it wrong to think the variance in both velocity and quality between successful companies is just as large if not larger than the delta between AI usage and no AI usage?

What about a conservative approach to AI adoption, looking for a moderate boost in velocity but maintaining most existing quality? Would that not be ideal? Or might it depend on the specific market the company operates in?
